title: -Contract No. 104289-1 Amendment: Jackson & Coker for Locums Tenens Psychiatrists

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to amend Contract No.
104289-1 with vendor Jackson & Coker Locum Tenens, LLC to provide locum tenens
psychiatrists in an amount of $500,000 for a not to exceed amount of $1,464,480
through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for the scope of work in the amount of $500,000 are currently available in the
Fiscal Year 2026 budget in Health (Short/Doyle) Fund 316.
sections.current_situation:
This service provides a safety net for consumers when the Mental Health Division’s
Adult Mental Health Clinic experiences a shortage of staff allowed to prescribe
medication. This contract amendment will allow intermittent usage of locum tenens
medication services through Jackson & Coker Locum Tenens, LLC to ensure clients
have access to medication. Locum tenens are licensed and qualified Psychiatrists or
Psychiatric Nurse Practitioners who have a temporary placement at the contracting
facility. The Mental Health Division is planning to submit a Request for Proposals for
this service before the end of the fiscal year and is requesting to amend this contract to
add sufficient funding to continue providing this service while that process takes place.
sections.background:
Over the last several years the Mental Health Division has experienced great difficulty in
filling vacant positions for staff who can prescribe psychiatric medications. Due to a
variety of factors, including the Medi-Cal expansion through the Affordable Care Act, an
existing shortage of psychiatrists in the region has been greatly exacerbated. By
contracting with a locum tenens, the City is able to provide constant services when
there is staffing turnover, long term vacancies or additional short-term staffing needs.
To provide necessary medication prescription and monitoring services while attempting to
fill staff vacancies, the City entered into Contract No. 9880 with J&C on March 17, 2015,

for locum tenens services with a contract limit of $24,900. When recruitment efforts did
not lead to permanent staffing, contract amendment 9880A increased the contract limit to
$49,900 on June 19, 2015. Due to continued staff vacancies and an approaching contract
limit, contract amendment 9880B was executed on July 24, 2015, to increase the contract
limit to $99,900. Because of ongoing staff shortages, contract amendment 9880C was
executed on August 24, 2016, to increase the contract limit to $174,900. When
recruitment efforts again did not lead to permanent staffing, on January 2, 2018, contract
amendment 9880D (ERMA Contract No. 104289-1) increased the contract limit to
$400,000.
Primarily due to staffing shortages throughout the region, and the significant increases
in hourly rates for locum tenens psychiatrists, on June 11, 2021, Contract No. 9880E
(ERMA Contract No. 104289-1) was executed to increase the amount by $564,480 for a
revised contract amount not to exceed $964,480. On Aug
sections.rationale:
Jackson & Coker offers locums tenens that provide psychiatric prescriber services to
Medi-Cal, uninsured, disenfranchised, and underserved populations. These services are
necessary for the provision of mental health treatment provided by the City. These
services will only be utilized during staff shortages and are not a replacement for
permanent staffing.
sections.alternatives:
The City is required to provide necessary medication prescription and monitoring
services for individuals with mental illness. A shorter contract period and a smaller
amount of funding were considered, but this would require the Mental Health Division to
return with more frequent amendments to this contract over time.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 104289-1 AMENDMENT WITH JACKSON & COKER FOR LOCUM
TENENS PSYCHIATRISTS
WHEREAS, Berkeley Mental Health (“BMH”) Division has a successful partnership with
Jackson & Coker Locum Tenens, LLC (“J&C”), providing locums tenens psychiatry
services for BMH clients; and
WHEREAS, J&C is an essential provider of psychiatry services in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, J&C offers locum tenens that provide psychiatry services, provided by
licensed psychiatrists, to Medi-Cal, uninsured, disenfranchised, and underserved
populations; and
WHEREAS, J&C’s services support mental health goals of improving the health and well-
being of the community during periods of staff shortages; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880 with J&C on March 17,
2015, for a term ending on June 30, 2016, for an expenditure contract limit not to exceed
$24,900, and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880A with J&C on June 19,
2015, for an expenditure contract not to exceed $49,900, and extended the term to June
30, 2017; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880B with J&C on July 24,
2015, by Resolution No. 67,174-N.S., for an expenditure contract increasing the amount
by $50,00 for a revised contract amount not to exceed $99,900; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880C with J&C on August
24, 2016, by Resolution No. 67,3034-N.S., for an expenditure contract increasing the
amount by $75,00 for a revised contract amount not to exceed $174,900; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880D (ERMA Contract No.
104289-1) with J&C on January 2, 2018, by Resolution No. 67,330-N.S., increasing the
amount by $225,100 for a revised contract amount not to exceed $400,000; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley entered into Contract No. 9880E (ERMA Contract No.
104289-1) with J&C on June 11, 2021, by Resolution No. 69,721-N.S., increasing the
amount
